name: Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit
name_original: Osornillo y Melgar de Fernamental — circular por Sendero de los Hortelanos (PRC-BU 190)
description: This difficult 15.4-mile (24.8 km) hiking circuit near Melgar de Fernamental takes over six hours and features the Church of the Assumption
region_intro: Hiking around Melgar De Fernamental offers routes through the open landscapes of the region. The terrain is generally flat, characterized by historical paths, agricultural fields, and the presence of the Canal de Castilla. Elevation gains are typically low, making many trails accessible. The area features a mix of historical aqueducts, castles, and sections of the Camino Francés. Best hiking trails around Melgar De Fernamental The most popular hiking route is Camino Francés: Itero de la Vega and Itero del Castillo Loop, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This easy route follows a section of the historic Camino Francés. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla from Osorno La Mayor by the Canal de Castilla, a moderate 11.9 miles (19.2 km) path. This route features views of the Canal de Castilla and the Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla. Local hikers also love the Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 6 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Melgar De Fernamental is defined by historical paths, canal-side trails, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Melgar De Fernamental?
A: The hiking trails around Melgar De Fernamental generally feature open, flat landscapes. You'll encounter historical paths, agricultural fields, and sections alongside the Canal de Castilla. Elevation gains are typically low, making many routes quite accessible.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, for an easy and historically rich walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067283796/camino-frances-itero-de-la-vega-and-itero-del-castillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Itero de la Vega and Itero del Castillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.9-mile (6.3 km) route follows a section of the famous Camino Francés and takes about 1 hour 37 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes in the area?
A: For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104394625/sendero-de-los-hortelanos-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a 15.4-mile (24.8 km) trail through open countryside, typically completed in about 6 hours 22 minutes. It offers a significant distance for those looking for a longer day out.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Melgar De Fernamental?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104394625/sendero-de-los-hortelanos-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093023077/acueducto-de-abanades-circular-desde-osorno-la-mayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de Abánades — circular desde Osorno la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.3-mile (11.7 km) loop.

Q: What historical sights or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/849948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás Hermitage at Puente Fitero&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7645783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hospital San Nicolas&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4289247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Rey Aqueduct (Canal de Castilla)&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093014484/castillo-de-las-cabanas-de-castilla-from-osorno-la-mayor-by-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla from Osorno La Mayor by the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, also feature views of historic castles.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Melgar De Fernamental suitable for families with children?
A: Given the generally flat terrain and low elevation gains, many trails are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067283796/camino-frances-itero-de-la-vega-and-itero-del-castillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Itero de la Vega and Itero del Castillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that could be a good option for families looking for a shorter, accessible walk.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Melgar De Fernamental?
A: The open landscapes of Melgar De Fernamental are pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very warm, and winter might bring colder conditions, though the low elevation makes year-round hiking possible with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Canal de Castilla?
A: Yes, the Canal de Castilla is a prominent feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093014484/castillo-de-las-cabanas-de-castilla-from-osorno-la-mayor-by-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla from Osorno La Mayor by the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; route offers excellent views along the canal.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Melgar De Fernamental?
A: The area is appreciated by the komoot community for its accessible trails and historical significance. Hikers often praise the quiet, open countryside and the opportunity to walk sections of the Camino Francés and explore the Canal de Castilla.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, Melgar De Fernamental and surrounding villages typically offer public parking options. It is advisable to check local maps or route descriptions for the most convenient parking spots near your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of castles?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093014484/castillo-de-las-cabanas-de-castilla-from-osorno-la-mayor-by-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla from Osorno La Mayor by the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; route provides views of the Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castrojeriz Castle loop from Villasilos&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic vistas of Castrojeriz Castle.
tips: A town located on the banks of the Pisuerga River, in which Plaza España or the Church of the Assumption of Our Lady stand out, a religious sanctuary of Melgar de Fernamental of great proportions dating from the 15th century.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit?
faqs.answer: The Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit is a challenging hike covering approximately 24.8 kilometers. On average, it takes about 6 hours and 20 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. While the elevation gain is moderate (around 107 meters), its significant length of nearly 25 kilometers contributes to its challenging classification. Hikers should be prepared for a long day on the trail.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the circuit?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and the presence of official routes like the Canal de Castilla and local roads, you can expect varied terrain. This may include sections along canal paths, rural tracks, and possibly some paved or unpaved local roads. The low elevation gain suggests it's not overly steep, but the distance requires endurance.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant highlight on this route is the Church of the Assumption of Our Lady, Melgar de Fernamental. This religious building offers a cultural and historical point of interest during your hike.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Sendero de Los Hortelanos, R7 - De los Navegantes, and the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur). Other smaller segments include R8 - De la Mota and R9 - Ruta de los Carros, as well as local streets like Calle Ribera del Pisuerga.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this circuit?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this circuit would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot in this region, making a long hike like this more strenuous. Winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially less favorable conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact circuit are not widely published, many rural trails in Spain are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or on shared paths. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to access the Sendero de los Hortelanos Circuit. It appears to be a publicly accessible route.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking and trailhead access for the circuit?
faqs.answer: As a circuit trail, it typically starts and ends in the same general area. Given the highlight in Melgar de Fernamental, it's likely that parking and trailhead access would be available in or near the town. Look for public parking areas or designated trail access points within Melgar de Fernamental.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a difficult, nearly 25-kilometer hike, you should pack essentials such as plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the weather. A map or GPS device (like komoot's app) is highly recommended, especially given the intersecting trails.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: Given that the route passes through or near Melgar de Fernamental, you should be able to find amenities such as cafes, pubs, or small shops within the town for refreshments or supplies before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Our Lady, Melgar de Fernamental
